task_completion.description: Verify that the Floreant POS application is launched and visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main Floreant POS application window or login screen is visible on the screen.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent accessed the Back Office/administrative area.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Floreant POS 'Back Office' dashboard or administrative menu is visible, indicating successful login (expected PIN: 1111).

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent opened the Configuration settings.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 'Configuration' or 'Settings' window/dialog is open within the Floreant POS application.

task_completion.description: Verify that the agent navigated to the Terminal settings section.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 'Terminal' tab or specific terminal configuration section is actively selected within the Configuration window.

task_completion.description: Verify that the terminal name was changed to 'Front Counter'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The terminal name input field explicitly contains the text 'Front Counter'. There should also be evidence of the agent saving or applying these changes (e.g., clicking 'OK', 'Apply', or returning to the main menu with the new name active).

integrity.description: Verify that the agent used the graphical user interface to complete the task.
integrity.visual_evidence: The screenshots show mouse clicks and keyboard inputs interacting directly with the Floreant POS GUI windows, rather than relying on terminal commands.

integrity.description: Verify that the agent did not bypass the GUI by directly editing the Derby database.
integrity.visual_evidence: There are no terminal windows open showing SQL commands, Derby CLI (ij), or scripts modifying the database at '/opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server'.

integrity.description: Verify that the interaction is with the actual Floreant POS software.
integrity.visual_evidence: The interface matches the standard Java-based Floreant POS application, not a text editor, mock UI, or generic configuration file.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target terminal name is exactly 'Front Counter'. The default PIN to access the Back Office administrative settings is '1111'. The task explicitly requires using the administrative settings (GUI) to rename the terminal. While the database is located at /opt/floreantpos/database/derby-server, direct database manipulation via CLI or scripts is a violation of the task instructions and should fail the integrity checks.
